query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on Lee Jae-myung's approval ratings for late June/early July 2026:

## Key Findings

**Was approval still falling?** It fell for ~6 consecutive weeks through late June, hitting record lows in both polls — but the **latest early-July readings show a modest rebound** in both. So the decline paused rather than continuing.

**Gallup Korea:**
- Bottomed at **51%** (Jun 24-26 poll) — lowest since inauguration
- Rebounded to **54%** approve / 36% disapprove (Jun 30-Jul 2, released Jul 3), +3pp
- **No national dead cross** (approval always led disapproval)

**Realmeter:**
- **First "dead cross" since inauguration** in the poll released **June 22, 2026** (46.7% approve vs 49.7% disapprove) — first time below 50% and first time disapproval exceeded approval since he took office June 4, 2025
- Continued negative: 46.5% (Jun 29 release), 6th straight weekly decline
- **Rebounded +0.5pp to 47.0%** (released Jul 6), first uptick in 7 weeks — but **still in dead-cross territory** (disapproval 49.2% still led)

**Drivers of the decline:** June 3 local election controversy + National Election Commission problems, ruling Democratic Party infighting, high won exchange rate (고환율), stock market plunge, and "excessive" welfare/spending concerns.

**Drivers of the July rebound:** Lee's "three mega-projects" (3대 메가 프로젝트) announcement plus policy signals cooling real estate, lifting stocks, and calming the exchange rate.

**Bottom line on "dead cross":** Yes — a genuine dead cross occurred in **Realmeter** (from the June 22 release), and it persisted through early July even as approval ticked up. **Gallup did not** register a national dead cross.

key_facts: Gallup Korea: Lee's approval hit 51% in the Jun 24-26 poll (released ~Jun 26, 2026), the lowest since he took office in June 2025.
Gallup rebounded to 54% approve / 36% disapprove in the Jun 30-Jul 2 poll (released Jul 3, 2026), a +3pp gain ending the decline.
Realmeter recorded the FIRST dead cross since inauguration in the poll released June 22, 2026 (fieldwork Jun 15-19): approval 46.7% vs disapproval 49.7%.
The June 22 Realmeter reading was the first time Lee's approval fell below 50% and the first time disapproval exceeded approval since he took office June 4, 2025.
Realmeter approval fell for six consecutive weeks; it was 46.5% (released Jun 29) then rose +0.5pp to 47.0% (released Jul 6, 2026), the first uptick in 7 weeks.
As of the July 6 Realmeter reading, disapproval (49.2%) still exceeded approval (47.0%) — still in dead-cross territory but narrowing.
Gallup did NOT register a national dead cross; its low was 51% approve vs ~41% disapprove.
Decline drivers: June 3 local election controversy and NEC problems, ruling Democratic Party infighting, high won exchange rate, stock market plunge, and 'excessive' welfare/spending concerns.
Early-July rebound drivers: Lee's 'three mega-projects' announcement and policy signals on real estate, stocks, and exchange rate.
Party support early July: Gallup DP 41% / PPP 26%; Realmeter DP 43.0% / PPP 40.3%; PPP briefly overtook DP in a mid-June Realmeter poll.
